response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack/web engineer with 20+ years of experience, but his background is almost entirely in web, mobile, and application development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, PHP). The role requires SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in his history. His closest relevant work (low-latency DLang services at Sociomantic, C/C++ at National Instruments) is over a decade old and application-level, not silicon or accelerator architecture. He meets the generic years-of-experience bar but not the domain requirements for a Principal Performance Architect.
strengths: 20+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded
Real low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching in DLang at millions of requests/sec)
Systems-level C/C++ background from National Instruments and audio DSP thesis work
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No deep learning training/inference optimization, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator workload background
Career trajectory is full-stack product engineering, far from principal-level performance architecture scope

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware co-design expertise
No AI/deep-learning workload or GPU performance experience
Long tenure with some low-latency systems work
